title: HCR/IF RNA-FISH combined protocol for the whole-mount brains of Drosophila melanogaster
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"63lel","text":"This is a protocol to perform RNA fluorescent in situ hybridization (RNA-FISH) using hybridization chain reaction (HCR) on whole-mount samples of the brains of the fly Drosophila melanogaster and other insects, e.g. the jumping ant Harpegnathos saltator. Probes and HCR reagents are purchased from Molecular Instruments. This protocol is loosely based on the \"generic sample in solution\" protocol published by Molecular Instruments. Our modifications include the description of fixation conditions, counterstaining by Hoechst, and altered washes. Additionally, we use larger concentrations of probes and hairpins following the protocol described by Younger, Herre et al. 2020. We have successfully employed this protocol to stain insect brains with up to 4 different probe sets simultaneously (hairpins conjugated with Alexa Fluor 488, 546, 496, and 647).","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":168,"length":23},{"style":"italic","offset":232,"length":21}],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":298,"length":21},{"key":1,"offset":359,"length":72},{"key":2,"offset":795,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"9rg11","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1kevi","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":3,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"9i7h","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"709B94A036A911ECBD30851F7CBC6798","url":"https://www.molecularinstruments.com/"}},"1":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"07BE124036AA11ECBD30851F7CBC6798","url":"https://files.molecularinstruments.com/MI-Protocol-RNAFISH-GenericSolution-Rev7.pdf"}},"2":{"type":"citation","mutability":"","data":{"authors":"Meg A. Younger, Margaret Herre, Alison R. Ehrlich, Zhongyan Gong, Zachary N. Gilbert, Saher Rahiel, Benjamin J. Matthews, Leslie B. Vosshall","can_edit":1,"date":"1604877540","doi":"https://doi.org/10.1101/2020.11.07.368720","guid":"49613CBE341F11EE9D4302C0B41BC903","journal":"bioRxiv","protocol_id":null,"title":"Non-canonical odor coding ensures unbreakable mosquito attraction to humans"}},"3":{"type":"citation","mutability":"","data":{"authors":"Meg A. Younger, Margaret Herre, Alison R. Ehrlich, Zhongyan Gong, Zachary N. Gilbert, Saher Rahiel, Benjamin J. Matthews, Leslie B. Vosshall","can_edit":1,"date":"1604877540","doi":"https://doi.org/10.1101/2020.11.07.368720","guid":"49613CBE341F11EE9D4302C0B41BC903","journal":"bioRxiv","protocol_id":null,"title":"Non-canonical odor coding ensures unbreakable mosquito attraction to humans"}}}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"8jdji","text":"WORKING PRACTICES:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":18}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"c6ba1","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6s4bn","text":"Prepare all buffers using nuclease-free water. Use filter tips and nuclease-free tubes. If using spot plates, pre-clean them first with household bleach diluted 1:10 in water and then with 70% ethanol. Wear gloves and adhere to other practices aimed at minimizing RNA degradation in the sample. Working in a clean bench is not required if other RNAse-free practice are followed.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fu1cl","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"hjh","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"74r34","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dffht","text":"PROBE DESIGN:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":13}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f7j4","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2d2nl","text":"We select the target sequence or isoform of the gene of interest and let Molecular Instruments design the probes. For genes with multiple isoforms, either target the isoform that includes as many as possible constitutive exons and as few as possible alternatively spliced exons, or the isoform that has the highest RNA-seq coverage (assessed visually in IGV) if RNA-seq data are available. Aim for the highest number of probes in a set, ideally 40, although we have successfully performed experiments with probe sets containing \u003c20 probes.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"69l1b","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"50m9f","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"e29nl","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fn5om","text":"AMPLIFIER CHOICE:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":17}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5et1h","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7v0dr","text":"We routinely perform multiplexed stainings with up to 4 different probe sets and a Hoechst counterstain. We use amplifiers conjugated with Alexa Fluor 488, 546, 594, and 647. We are able to detect clearly distinguishable signals with minimal bleed-through on our confocal microscope (Leica SP8). However, be aware that simultaneously using fluorophores with partially overlapping spectra (e.g. AF 546 and 594) requires setting narrower detection ranges, which reduces the amount of signal detected.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"4cbks","text":"This protocol uses solutions of paraformaldehyde and formamide, which are highly toxic chemicals. Consult the SDS sheets of the reagents used in this protocol for proper handling instructions.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Pre-heat an aliquot of Probe Hybridization Buffer if proceeding with hybridization on the same day (see steps below) 250 µL, / sample (If preparing a new probe mixture) 110 µL, / sample (If reusing probes) 37 °C
Remove excess 2° Antibody solution by washing 5 x 5 min with 150 μL of 0.3% PBST 00:05:00 00:05:00 00:05:00 00:05:00 00:05:00 Room temperature 24 rpm, °C, , Nutator

Pre-hybridize samples by incubating them with 100 μL of warm Probe Hybridization Buffer from step 2. Pipet several times to ensure the brains are submerged in Probe Hybridization Buffer instead of residual PBTx. 00:30:00 37 °C Hybridization buffer is viscous, and the brains might be stuck on the wall or float to the surface. Make sure you see where the brains are during this step. Be careful not to remove them or let them dry out by accident.

In the meantime, prepare a 8nM probe solution by adding 0.4 pmol of each probe mixture (e.g. 0.4 μL of 1 μM stock) to the warm Probe Hybridization Buffer for the total volume of 100 μL. 0.4 µL, 1µM probe per sample 100 µL, Warm probe hybridization buffer per sample If the signal is weak, consider increase the probe concentration by 2X - 4X.
Remove the pre-hybridization solution from the sample and add the probe solution from step 18. Take extra care while removing the liquid. Hybridization Buffer is viscous and brains may float.
Incubate samples with the probes. Overnight, We usually do ~24 h, but incubation can be extended to ~48 h. Minimum recommended is 12 h 37 °C 24 rpm, °C, , Nutator
Pre-heat an aliquot of Probe Wash Buffer 37 °C 450 µL, per sample

Equilibrate an aliquot of Amplification Buffer to room temperature Room temperature 60 µL, per amplifier per sample (if preparing new) + 110 µL, per sample If using 3 different amplifiers for 2 samples, 60 * 3 * 2 + 110 * 2 = 580µL is to be equilibrated.

Remove excess probes by washing 4 x 10 min with 100µL pre-heated Probe Wash Buffer from step 22 00:10:00 Wash 100µL pre-heated probe wash buffer 00:10:00 Wash 100µL pre-heated probe wash buffer 00:10:00 Wash 100µL pre-heated probe wash buffer 00:10:00 Wash 100µL pre-heated probe wash buffer 37 °C 24 rpm, °C, , Nutator

Prepare separately each hairpin (h1 and h2) of each amplifier. Each sample require 3 μL of each 3 μM stock of hairpin to be mixed in a final volume of 100µL/sample amplification buffer. Aliquot the required amount of hairpin to individual PCR tubes. For example, if amplifiers B1 and B2 are being used, prepare 4 PCR tubes that contain B1-h1, B1-h2, B2-h1, and B2-h2, respectively. Incubate the tubes in a thermocycler at 95 ºC for 90 sec. Immediately take them out of the machine (while it is still at 95 ºC), place them in a rack and incubate them at room temperature in the dark for at least 30 min. 00:30:00, or longer Room temperature For lowly-expressed targets, consider increase the amplifier concentration by 2X.
Equlibrate each sample with 100 μL of Amplification Buffer 00:10:00, can be extended to 30 min Room temperature 24 rpm, °C, , Nutator or 60 rpm, °C, , Orbital shaker
Briefly spin down the hairpin solutions, add 100 µL/sample amplification buffer, add 2 μL of each hairpin into the 100 µL amplification buffer to make the final mixture. Room temperature
Remove 100 μL liquid from the samples and add the Amplification Buffer + hairpins from step 29 Take extra care while removing the liquid. Amplification Buffer is viscous and the brain may float.
Incubate in the dark. Overnight Room temperature 24 rpm, °C, , Nutator or 60 rpm, °C, , Orbital shaker
Save hairpin mixture and keep at -20°C Hairpin mixture can be reused for > 3 times without apparent decrease of signal.
Wash 2 x 5 min and 2 x 15 min with 150 μL of 5X SSCT 00:05:00 00:05:00 00:15:00 00:15:00 Room temperature 24 rpm, °C, , Nutator

If not using DAPI or GFP/RFP booster, skip this step and the next and proceed to step 37. If using DAPI: incubate samples with 150 μL of SSCT + DAPI (1:500 from 5 µg/µL stock) at room temperature for 1hr. If using GFP/RFP booster, add 0.3µL to the butter above (1:500 dilution). Room temperature 01:00:00 24 rpm, °C, , Nutator or 60 rpm, °C, , Orbital shaker DAPI and GFP/RFP booster incubation works well with 1h RT or 4 degree overnight.
All the next steps are light sensitive and must be done in the dark!
Wash samples 2 × 5min with 1mL of 5X SSCT at room temperature. 00:05:00 5x SSCT 00:05:00 5x SSCT
During sample equilibration, warm up the thermo cycler to 95°C.

Remove probe solution and store it at -20°C. Take extra care while removing the liquid. Hybridization Buffer is viscous and brains may float. Probe solution can be reused for > 3 times without apparent decrease
Dissect brains in cold Schneider's medium Dissection can also be done in 1X Nuclease-Free PBS.
Transfer the brains to a PCR tube and rinse 3x with 150 μL of 0.3% PBST Make 0.3% PBST using 1X Nuclease-Free PBS. Room temperature

Fix brains in 500 μL of 4% PFA / Schneider's medium in a spot plate 00:20:00 Room temperature 24 rpm, °C, , Nutator 60 rpm, °C, , Orbital Shaker For fixation and all subsequent steps, samples can be placed either in Eppendorf tubes or in wells of a spot plate (e.g. Pyrex spot plate with 9 depressions, Catalog #CLS722085). Tubes are incubated on a nutator and plates are incubated on an orbital (horizontal) shaker.
Pre-warm HCR Antibody Buffer to room temperature 300 µL, per sample
Prepare working concentration of 1° antibodies in HCR Antibody Buffer. Prepare 100 μL per sample

Remove HCR Antibody Buffer and add 100 μL of 1° antibody solution to sample; incubation sample over two nights at 4°C with gentle rotation 4 °C 24 rpm, °C, , Nutator Overnight For fly optical lobe, primary antibody incubation for two nights is optimal; can be adjusted based on the tissue types
Block samples with 200 μL of HCR Antibody Buffer for 2-4h at 4°C Blocking time might vary, suggestion for minimum is 1.5h, >2h for optimal signal-to-noise quality
Wash 3x 15 min with 150 μL of 0.3% PBST Room temperature 00:15:00 00:15:00 00:15:00 24 rpm, °C, , Nutator

Remove excess antibodies by washing 4x30 min with 150 μL of 0.3% PBST at room temperature Room temperature 00:30:00 00:30:00 00:30:00 00:30:00 24 rpm, °C, , Nutator 1° Antibody solution can be reused for up to 2-3 times.
Remove PBST and add HCR 2° Antibody Probe solution to samples; incubate samples over two nights at 4°C with gentle rotation 4 °C 24 rpm, °C, , Nutator Overnight

Prepare working concentration of HCR Antibody Probe with specific amplifier in 100 μL of HCR Antibody Buffer; antibody probe e.g. anti-mouse B1 Plan out the use of amplifiers for all antibodies and HCR RNA probes for the multiplex experiment; avoid overlap across all antibodies and RNA probes
Pre-warm HCR Antibody Buffer to room temperature 100 µL, per sample
Remove PBST and post-fix sample with 500 μL of 4% PFA, incubate for 10 min at room temperature Use 500 μL of 4% PFA if transferring back to glass dish, use 200 μL instead if fixing in tube.
Remove fixative and rinse sample quickly 3x with 150 μL of 0.3% PBST
Wash 3 x 10 min with 150 μL of 5X SSCT 00:10:00 00:10:00 00:10:00 Room temperature 24 rpm, °C, , Nutator
Rinse with 2X Nuclease-Free PBS to remove the detergent; store in PBS with foil to protect sample from light or proceed to mounting and imaging.
